ccessor is an instance of a class definition. Accessor classes are object-oriented, using a Java-style, where there is a single base class and any number of implemented interfaces. An accessor class definition is an XML file whose top-level element

definition is an XML file whose top-level element is "class", with a syntax like this: < class name =" accessorName " extends =" baseClassName " implements = interface1, interface2, ... > ... body of the definition ... </class> NOTE: The implements

the definition ... </class> NOTE: The implements attribute is not yet implemented in any host. < interface name =" interfaceName " implements = interface1, interface2, ... > ... body of the definition ... </interface> Back to accessor specification
